raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint regarding a {$5400.00} charge on my American Express card ending in 2019 from a New York establishment called XXXX  XXXX. I did not knowingly or voluntarily authorize this charge. 

I visited the establishment briefly and only recall paying a small cover charge and purchasing a few drinks. Beyond that, I have no memory of agreeing to a multi-thousand-dollar transaction. I was never shown an itemized receipt or informed of the total amount being charged. When I awoke the next morning, multiple credit cards were missing from my possession, including the one used for this transaction. 

The merchant claims to have a signed billing authorization, but American Express has not provided me with a copy despite multiple requests. A signature alone does not constitute valid, informed consent, especially given the circumstances of possible intoxication or exploitation. 

Additionally, multiple unauthorized high-value charges occurred on other credit cards ( including my XXXX card ) during the same evening and at the same establishment, indicating a clear pattern of misuse and exploitation. 

American Express denied my dispute despite the lack of evidence showing informed consent, disclosure of pricing, or proof of mental capacity at the time of signing. I have requested second-level review and provided documentation showing the pattern of multi-card misuse, but American Express continues to uphold the charge. 

I am requesting that the CFPB review this matter and ensure that American Express complies with cardholder-protection regulations and reopens this dispute for proper review.
